[[画像:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|桂離宮庭園]] '''回遊式庭園'''（かいゆうしきていえん）は、[[日本庭園]]の形式のひとつで、園内を回遊して鑑賞する[[庭園]]。園内を回遊する形式の庭園は日本以外にも存在するが、回遊式庭園という語は主に日本庭園のみを指して用いられる。
== 概要 == 回遊式庭園は、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]によって造営された[[大名庭園]]において発達した形式であり、日本庭園の集大成とも位置づけられる。
最も一般的な形式は'''池泉回遊式庭園'''（ちせんかいゆうしきていえん）と呼ばれるもので、大きな池を中心に配し、その周囲に園路を巡らして、[[築山]]、池中に設けた小島、橋、名石などで各地の景勝などを再現した。園路の所々には、散策中の休憩所として、また、庭園を眺望する展望所として、茶亭、東屋などが設けられた。
== 代表的な回遊式庭園 == <!--皇室関係の代表的庭園、特別史跡、特別名勝、日本三名園を挙げています--> * [[桂離宮]]庭園（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[西京区]]） * [[京都御所]]庭園（京都府京都市[[上京区]]） * [[小石川後楽園]]（[[東京都]][[文京区]]） - [[史跡#特別史跡|特別史跡]]・[[名勝|特別名勝]] * [[浜離宮恩賜庭園]]（東京都[[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[偕楽園]]（[[茨城県]][[水戸市]]） - [[史跡]]・[[名勝]] * [[兼六園]]（[[石川県]][[金沢市]]） - 特別名勝 * [[後楽園]]（[[岡山県]][[岡山市]]） - 特別名勝
{{DEFAULTSORT:かいゆうしきていえん}} [[Category:日本庭園]] {{Japan-culture-stub}}

[[画像:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|桂離宮庭園]] '''回遊式庭園'''（かいゆうしきていえん）は、[[日本庭園]]の形式のひとつで、園内を回遊して鑑賞する[[庭園]]。園内を回遊する形式の庭園は日本以外にも存在するが、回遊式庭園という語は主に日本庭園のみを指して用いられる。

== 代表的な回遊式庭園 == <!--特別史跡、特別名勝、日本三名園級の代表的庭園を挙げています--> * [[桂離宮]]庭園（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[西京区]]） * [[京都御所]]庭園（京都府京都市[[上京区]]） * [[小石川後楽園]]（[[東京都]][[文京区]]） - [[史跡#特別史跡|特別史跡]]・[[名勝|特別名勝]] * [[浜離宮恩賜庭園]]（東京都[[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[一乗谷朝倉氏遺跡|一乗谷朝倉氏庭園]]（[[福井県]][[福井市]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[偕楽園]]（[[茨城県]][[水戸市]]） - [[史跡]]・[[名勝]] * [[兼六園]]（[[石川県]][[金沢市]]） - 特別名勝 * [[後楽園]]（[[岡山県]][[岡山市]]） - 特別名勝 * [[玄宮園]]（[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]） - 特別史跡・名勝 * [[足立美術館]]庭園（[[島根県]][[安来市]]） * [[栗林公園]]（[[香川県]][[高松市]]） - 特別名勝
{{DEFAULTSORT:かいゆうしきていえん}} [[Category:日本庭園]] {{Japan-culture-stub}}

[[画像:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|桂離宮庭園]] '''回遊式庭園'''（かいゆうしきていえん）は、[[日本庭園]]の形式のひとつで、園内を回遊して鑑賞する[[庭園]]。園内を回遊する形式の庭園は日本以外にも存在するが、回遊式庭園という語は主に日本庭園のみを指して用いられる。
== 概要 == 回遊式庭園は、[[室町時代]]における[[禅宗]][[寺院]]や[[江戸時代]]においては[[大名]]により多く造営された形式で、日本庭園の集大成とも位置づけられる。
最も一般的な形式は'''池泉回遊式庭園'''（ちせんかいゆうしきていえん）と呼ばれるもので、大きな池を中心に配し、その周囲に園路を巡らして、[[築山]]、池中に設けた小島、橋、名石などで各地の景勝などを再現した。園路の所々には、散策中の休憩所として、また、庭園を眺望する展望所として、茶亭、東屋なども設けられた。

[[画像:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|桂離宮庭園]] '''回遊式庭園'''（かいゆうしきていえん）は、[[日本庭園]]の形式のひとつで、園内を回遊して鑑賞する[[庭園]]。園内を回遊する形式の庭園は日本以外にも存在するが、回遊式庭園という語は主に日本庭園のみを指して用いられる。

== 代表的な回遊式庭園 == <!--特別史跡、特別名勝、日本三名園級の代表的庭園を挙げています--> * [[偕楽園]]（[[茨城県]][[水戸市]]） - [[史跡]]・[[名勝]] * [[小石川後楽園]]（[[東京都]][[文京区]]） - [[史跡#特別史跡|特別史跡]]・[[名勝|特別名勝]] * [[浜離宮恩賜庭園]]（東京都[[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[兼六園]]（[[石川県]][[金沢市]]） - 特別名勝 * [[一乗谷朝倉氏遺跡|一乗谷朝倉氏庭園]]（[[福井県]][[福井市]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[玄宮園]]（[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]） - 特別史跡・名勝 * [[桂離宮]]庭園（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[西京区]]） - [[宮内庁]]管理 * [[京都御所]]庭園（京都府京都市[[上京区]]） - 宮内庁管理 * [[鹿苑寺|鹿苑寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[北区 (京都市)|北区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝・[[世界遺産]] * [[慈照寺|慈照寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[左京区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[天龍寺|天龍寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[右京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[西芳寺|西芳寺庭園]]（京都府京都市西京区） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[醍醐寺三宝院庭園]]（京都府京都市[[伏見区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[旧大乗院庭園]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]） - [[名勝]] * [[依水園]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]） - [[名勝]] * [[当麻寺]]中之坊庭園（[[奈良県]][[葛城市]]） - [[名勝]] * [[和歌山城西之丸庭園]]（[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]） - [[名勝]] * [[養翠園]]（[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]） - [[名勝]] * [[六三園]]（[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]）- [[登録有形文化財]] * [[温山荘園]]（[[和歌山県]][[海南市]]） - [[名勝]] * [[根来寺|根来寺庭園]]（[[和歌山県]][[岩出市]]） - 名勝 * [[長久邸]]（[[和歌山県]][[海南市]]） * [[後楽園]]（[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]） - 特別名勝 * [[足立美術館]]庭園（[[島根県]][[安来市]]） * [[栗林公園]]（[[香川県]][[高松市]]） - 特別名勝 * <!--薩摩藩統治時代の琉球庭園-->[[識名園]]（[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]） - 特別名勝・世界遺産
== 関連項目 == *[[大名庭園]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かいゆうしきていえん}} [[Category:日本庭園|*]] {{Japan-culture-stub}}

revision.text:
[[画像:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|桂離宮庭園]] '''回遊式庭園'''（かいゆうしきていえん）は、[[日本庭園]]の形式のひとつで、園内を回遊して鑑賞する[[庭園]]。園内を回遊する形式の庭園は日本以外にも存在するが、回遊式庭園という語は主に日本庭園のみを指して用いられる。
== 概要 == 回遊式庭園は、[[室町時代]]における[[禅宗]][[寺院]]や[[江戸時代]]においては[[大名]]により多く造営された形式で、日本庭園の集大成とも位置づけられる。
最も一般的な形式は'''池泉回遊式庭園'''（ちせんかいゆうしきていえん）と呼ばれるもので、大きな池を中心に配し、その周囲に園路を巡らして、[[築山]]、池中に設けた小島、橋、[[庭石|名石]]などで各地の[[景勝]]などを再現した。園路の所々には、散策中の休憩所として、また、庭園を眺望する展望所として、[[茶室|茶亭]]、[[四阿|東屋]]なども設けられた。
== 代表的な回遊式庭園 == <!--特別史跡、特別名勝、日本三名園級の代表的庭園を挙げています--> * [[小石川後楽園]]（[[東京都]][[文京区]]） - [[史跡#特別史跡|特別史跡]]・[[日本の特別名勝一覧|特別名勝]] * [[浜離宮恩賜庭園]]（東京都[[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[兼六園]]（[[石川県]][[金沢市]]） - 特別名勝 * [[一乗谷朝倉氏遺跡|一乗谷朝倉氏庭園]]（[[福井県]][[福井市]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[玄宮園]]（[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]） - 特別史跡・名勝 * [[桂離宮]]庭園（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[西京区]]） - [[宮内庁]]管理 * [[鹿苑寺|鹿苑寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[北区 (京都市)|北区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝・[[世界遺産]] * [[慈照寺|慈照寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[左京区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[天龍寺|天龍寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[右京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[西芳寺|西芳寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[西京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[二条城]]二之丸庭園（京都府京都市[[中京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[醍醐寺三宝院庭園]]（京都府京都市[[伏見区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[足立美術館]]庭園（[[島根県]][[安来市]]）<!--米国の日本庭園専門雑誌『ジャーナル・オブ・ジャパニーズ・ガーデニング』が行っている日本庭園ランキングで1位--> * [[後楽園]]（[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]） - 特別名勝 * [[栗林公園]]（[[香川県]][[高松市]]） - 特別名勝 * <!--薩摩藩統治時代の琉球庭園-->[[識名園]]（[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]） - 特別名勝・世界遺産
== 関連項目 == *[[大名庭園]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かいゆうしきていえん}} [[Category:日本庭園|*]] {{Japan-culture-stub}}

最も一般的な形式は'''池泉回遊式庭園'''（ちせんかいゆうしきていえん）と呼ばれるもので、大きな池を中心に配し、その周囲に園路を巡らして、[[築山]]、池中に設けた小島、橋、[[庭石|名石]]などで各地の[[景勝]]などを再現した。園路の所々には、散策中の休憩所として、また、庭園を眺望する展望所として、[[茶室|茶亭]]、[[四阿|東屋]]なども設けられた。
== 代表的な回遊式庭園 == <!--特別史跡、特別名勝、日本三名園級の代表的庭園を挙げています--> * [[小石川後楽園]]（[[東京都]][[文京区]]） - [[史跡#特別史跡|特別史跡]]・[[日本の特別名勝一覧|特別名勝]] *[[六義園]]（[[東京都]][[文京区]]） - [[特別名勝]] * [[浜離宮恩賜庭園]]（東京都[[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[兼六園]]（[[石川県]][[金沢市]]） - 特別名勝 * [[一乗谷朝倉氏遺跡|一乗谷朝倉氏庭園]]（[[福井県]][[福井市]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[玄宮園]]（[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]） - 特別史跡・名勝 * [[桂離宮]]庭園（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[西京区]]） - [[宮内庁]]管理 * [[鹿苑寺|鹿苑寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[北区 (京都市)|北区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝・[[世界遺産]] * [[慈照寺|慈照寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[左京区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[天龍寺|天龍寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[右京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[西芳寺|西芳寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[西京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[二条城]]二之丸庭園（京都府京都市[[中京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[醍醐寺三宝院庭園]]（京都府京都市[[伏見区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[足立美術館]]庭園（[[島根県]][[安来市]]）<!--米国の日本庭園専門雑誌『ジャーナル・オブ・ジャパニーズ・ガーデニング』が行っている日本庭園ランキングで1位--> * [[後楽園]]（[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]） - 特別名勝 * [[栗林公園]]（[[香川県]][[高松市]]） - 特別名勝 * <!--薩摩藩統治時代の琉球庭園-->[[識名園]]（[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]） - 特別名勝・世界遺産

== 代表的な回遊式庭園 == <!--特別史跡、特別名勝、日本三名園級の代表的庭園を挙げています--> * [[小石川後楽園]]（[[東京都]][[文京区]]） - [[史跡#特別史跡|特別史跡]]・[[日本の特別名勝一覧|特別名勝]] *[[六義園]]（[[東京都]][[文京区]]） - [[特別名勝]] * [[浜離宮恩賜庭園]]（東京都[[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[兼六園]]（[[石川県]][[金沢市]]） - 特別名勝 * [[一乗谷朝倉氏遺跡|一乗谷朝倉氏庭園]]（[[福井県]][[福井市]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[玄宮園]]（[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]） - 特別史跡・名勝 * [[桂離宮]]庭園（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[西京区]]） - [[宮内庁]]管理 * [[鹿苑寺|鹿苑寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[北区 (京都市)|北区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝・[[世界遺産]] * [[慈照寺|慈照寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[左京区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[天龍寺|天龍寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[右京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[西芳寺|西芳寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[西京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[二条城]]二之丸庭園（京都府京都市[[中京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[醍醐寺三宝院庭園]]（京都府京都市[[伏見区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[足立美術館]]庭園（[[島根県]][[安来市]]）<!--米国の日本庭園専門雑誌『ジャーナル・オブ・ジャパニーズ・ガーデニング』が行っている日本庭園ランキングで1位--> * [[後楽園]]（[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]） - 特別名勝 * [[栗林公園]]（[[香川県]][[高松市]]） - 特別名勝 * <!--薩摩藩統治時代の琉球庭園-->[[識名園]]（[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]） - 特別名勝・世界遺産
== 関連項目 == *[[大名庭園]] *[[池泉庭園]]

[[画像:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|桂離宮庭園]] '''回遊式庭園'''（かいゆうしきていえん）は、[[日本庭園]]の形式のひとつで、園内を回遊して鑑賞する[[庭園]]。園内を回遊する形式の庭園は日本以外にも存在するが、回遊式庭園という語は主に日本庭園のみを指して用いられる。
== 概要 == 回遊式庭園は、[[室町時代]]における[[禅宗]][[寺院]]や[[江戸時代]]においては[[大名]]により多く造営された形式で、日本庭園の集大成とも位置づけられる。
最も一般的な形式は'''[[池泉庭園|池泉]]回遊式庭園'''（ちせんかいゆうしきていえん）と呼ばれるもので、大きな池を中心に配し、その周囲に園路を巡らして、[[築山]]、池中に設けた小島、橋、[[庭石|名石]]などで各地の[[景勝]]などを再現した。園路の所々には、散策中の休憩所として、また、庭園を眺望する展望所として、[[茶室|茶亭]]、[[四阿|東屋]]なども設けられた。
== 代表的な回遊式庭園 == <!--特別史跡、特別名勝、日本三名園級の代表的庭園を挙げています--> * [[小石川後楽園]]（[[東京都]][[文京区]]） - [[史跡#特別史跡|特別史跡]]・[[日本の特別名勝一覧|特別名勝]] *[[六義園]]（[[東京都]][[文京区]]） - [[特別名勝]] * [[浜離宮恩賜庭園]]（東京都[[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[兼六園]]（[[石川県]][[金沢市]]） - 特別名勝 * [[一乗谷朝倉氏遺跡|一乗谷朝倉氏庭園]]（[[福井県]][[福井市]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[玄宮園]]（[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]） - 特別史跡・名勝 * [[桂離宮]]庭園（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[西京区]]） - [[宮内庁]]管理 * [[鹿苑寺|鹿苑寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[北区 (京都市)|北区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝・[[世界遺産]] * [[慈照寺|慈照寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[左京区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[天龍寺|天龍寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[右京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[西芳寺|西芳寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[西京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[二条城]]二之丸庭園（京都府京都市[[中京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[醍醐寺三宝院庭園]]（京都府京都市[[伏見区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[足立美術館]]庭園（[[島根県]][[安来市]]）<!--米国の日本庭園専門雑誌『ジャーナル・オブ・ジャパニーズ・ガーデニング』が行っている日本庭園ランキングで1位--> * [[後楽園]]（[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]） - 特別名勝 * [[栗林公園]]（[[香川県]][[高松市]]） - 特別名勝 * <!--薩摩藩統治時代の琉球庭園-->[[識名園]]（[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]） - 特別名勝・世界遺産
== 関連項目 == *[[大名庭園]] *[[池泉庭園]]

revision.text:
{{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * {{ill|Rikugien Garden|ja|六義園}} (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins|ja|一乗谷朝倉氏遺跡}} ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * {{ill|Kōrakuen|ja|後楽園}} ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Shikina-en|ja|識名園}} ({{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園}} * {{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}
{{Gardens and Gardening}}
[[Category:Japanese Gardens]] [[Category:Edo Period Gardens]] [[ja:回遊式庭園]]

'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * {{ill|Rikugien Garden|ja|六義園}} (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins|ja|一乗谷朝倉氏遺跡}} ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * {{ill|Kōrakuen|ja|後楽園}} ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Shikina-en|ja|識名園}} ({{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site

'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * {{ill|Rikugien Garden|ja|六義園}} (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins|ja|一乗谷朝倉氏遺跡}} ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * {{ill|Kōrakuen|ja|後楽園}} ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Shikina-en|ja|識名園}} ({{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園}} * {{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}

{{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * {{ill|Rikugien Garden|ja|六義園}} (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - {{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * {{ill|Rikugien Garden|ja|六義園}} (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins|ja|一乗谷朝倉氏遺跡}} ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * {{ill|Kōrakuen|ja|後楽園}} ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Shikina-en|ja|識名園}} ({{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園}} * {{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}

Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * [[Kōraku-en]] ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Shikina-en|ja|識名園}} ({{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site

{{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - {{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * [[Kōraku-en]] ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - {{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - '''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * [[Kōraku-en]] ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Shikina-en|ja|識名園}} ({{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園}} * {{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}

'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * [[Kōraku-en]] ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Shikina-en|ja|識名園}} ({{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site
== See Also == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園}} * {{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}

'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * [[Kōraku-en]] ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Shikina-en|ja|識名園}} ({{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site
== See Also == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園}} * {{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}

{{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * [[Kōraku-en]] ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Shikina-en|ja|識名園}} ({{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site
== See Also == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園}} * {{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}

{{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * [[Kōraku-en]] ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Shikina-en|ja|識名園}} ({{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site
== See Also == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園}} * {{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}

revision.text:
{{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * [[Kōraku-en]] ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Shikina-en|ja|識名園}} ({{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site
== See Also == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園}} * {{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}

'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.

{{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * [[Kōraku-en]] ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Shikina-en|ja|識名園}} ({{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site
== See Also == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園}} * {{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}

revision.text:
{{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * [[Kōraku-en]] ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Shikina-en|ja|識名園}} ({{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site
== See Also == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園}} * {{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}

'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Ginkaku-ji|ja|慈照寺}} Garden ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Tenryū-ji|ja|天龍寺}} Garden ({{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|ja|西芳寺}} Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} Ninomaru Garden ({{ill|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|中京区}}, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園}} at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} ({{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|ja|足立美術館}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * [[Kōraku-en]] ({{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|ja|北区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Shikina-en|ja|識名園}} ({{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site== See Also == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園}} * {{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}

{{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} in {{ill|Zen Buddhism|ja|禅宗}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Pond Strolling Garden|ja|池泉庭園}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|teahouses|ja|茶室}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Kōrakuen|ja|小石川後楽園}} ({{ill|Bunkyō, Tokyo|ja|文京区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|ja|浜離宮恩賜庭園}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|ja|中央区 (東京都)}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|ja|兼六園}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ({{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園}} ({{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|ja|桂離宮}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|西京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}) - Managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|ja|鹿苑寺}} Garden ({{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}}, Kyoto) - revision.text:
{{Wikidatabox}} [[画像:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|桂離宮庭園]] '''回遊式庭園'''（かいゆうしきていえん）は、[[日本庭園]]の形式のひとつで、園内を回遊して鑑賞する[[庭園]]。園内を回遊する形式の庭園は日本以外にも存在するが、回遊式庭園という語は主に日本庭園のみを指して用いられる。
== 概要 == 回遊式庭園は、[[室町時代]]における[[禅宗]][[寺院]]や[[江戸時代]]においては[[大名]]により多く造営された形式で、日本庭園の集大成とも位置づけられる。
最も一般的な形式は'''[[池泉庭園|池泉]]回遊式庭園'''（ちせんかいゆうしきていえん）と呼ばれるもので、大きな池を中心に配し、その周囲に園路を巡らして、[[築山]]、池中に設けた小島、橋、[[庭石|名石]]などで各地の[[景勝]]などを再現した。園路の所々には、散策中の休憩所として、また、庭園を眺望する展望所として、[[茶室|茶亭]]、[[四阿|東屋]]なども設けられた。
== 代表的な回遊式庭園 == <!--特別史跡、特別名勝、日本三名園級の代表的庭園を挙げています--> * [[小石川後楽園]]（[[東京都]][[文京区]]） - [[史跡#特別史跡|特別史跡]]・[[日本の特別名勝一覧|特別名勝]] *[[六義園]]（[[東京都]][[文京区]]） - [[特別名勝]] * [[浜離宮恩賜庭園]]（東京都[[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[兼六園]]（[[石川県]][[金沢市]]） - 特別名勝 * [[一乗谷朝倉氏遺跡|一乗谷朝倉氏庭園]]（[[福井県]][[福井市]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[玄宮園]]（[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]） - 特別史跡・名勝 * [[桂離宮]]庭園（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[西京区]]） - [[宮内庁]]管理 * [[鹿苑寺|鹿苑寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[北区 (京都市)|北区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝・[[世界遺産]] * [[慈照寺|慈照寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[左京区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[天龍寺|天龍寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[右京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[西芳寺|西芳寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[西京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[二条城]]二之丸庭園（京都府京都市[[中京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[醍醐寺三宝院庭園]]（京都府京都市[[伏見区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[足立美術館]]庭園（[[島根県]][[安来市]]）<!--米国の日本庭園専門雑誌『ジャーナル・オブ・ジャパニーズ・ガーデニング』が行っている日本庭園ランキングで1位--> * [[後楽園]]（[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]） - 特別名勝 * [[栗林公園]]（[[香川県]][[高松市]]） - 特別名勝 * <!--薩摩藩統治時代の琉球庭園-->[[識名園]]（[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]） - 特別名勝・世界遺産
== 関連項目 == *[[大名庭園]] *[[池泉庭園]]
{{庭と庭園、園芸とガーデニング}}

== 概要 == 回遊式庭園は、[[室町時代]]における[[禅宗]][[寺院]]や[[江戸時代]]においては[[大名]]により多く造営された形式で、日本庭園の集大成とも位置づけられる。
最も一般的な形式は'''[[池泉庭園|池泉]]回遊式庭園'''（ちせんかいゆうしきていえん）と呼ばれるもので、大きな池を中心に配し、その周囲に園路を巡らして、[[築山]]、池中に設けた小島、橋、[[庭石|名石]]などで各地の[[景勝]]などを再現した。園路の所々には、散策中の休憩所として、また、庭園を眺望する展望所として、[[茶室|茶亭]]、[[四阿|東屋]]なども設けられた。
== 代表的な回遊式庭園 == <!--特別史跡、特別名勝、日本三名園級の代表的庭園を挙げています--> * [[小石川後楽園]]（[[東京都]][[文京区]]） - [[史跡#特別史跡|特別史跡]]・[[日本の特別名勝一覧|特別名勝]] *[[六義園]]（[[東京都]][[文京区]]） - [[特別名勝]] * [[浜離宮恩賜庭園]]（東京都[[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[兼六園]]（[[石川県]][[金沢市]]） - 特別名勝 * [[一乗谷朝倉氏遺跡|一乗谷朝倉氏庭園]]（[[福井県]][[福井市]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[玄宮園]]（[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]） - 特別史跡・名勝 * [[桂離宮]]庭園（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[西京区]]） - [[宮内庁]]管理 * [[鹿苑寺|鹿苑寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[北区 (京都市)|北区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝・[[世界遺産]] * [[慈照寺|慈照寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[左京区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[天龍寺|天龍寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[右京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[西芳寺|西芳寺庭園]]（京都府京都市[[西京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[二条城]]二之丸庭園（京都府京都市[[中京区]]） - 史跡・特別名勝・世界遺産 * [[醍醐寺三宝院庭園]]（京都府京都市[[伏見区]]） - 特別史跡・特別名勝 * [[足立美術館]]庭園（[[島根県]][[安来市]]）<!--米国の日本庭園専門雑誌『ジャーナル・オブ・ジャパニーズ・ガーデニング』が行っている日本庭園ランキングで1位--> * [[後楽園]]（[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]） - 特別名勝 * [[栗林公園]]（[[香川県]][[高松市]]） - 特別名勝 * <!--薩摩藩統治時代の琉球庭園-->[[識名園]]（[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]） - 特別名勝・世界遺産
== 関連項目 == *[[大名庭園]] *[[池泉庭園]]
{{庭と庭園、園芸とガーデニング}}

revision.text:
{{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]] designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] in [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] temples and were extensively developed by [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''[[:en:Water garden|Pond Strolling Garden]]''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/artificial hills|qq=}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ornamental stones|qq=}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as [[:en:Chashitsu|teahouses]] and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/azumaya|qq=}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* [[:en:Koishikawa-Kōrakuen|Koishikawa Kōrakuen]] ([[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|qq=}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Place of Scenic Beauty|qq=}} * [[Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Hama-rikyū Gardens|Hama-rikyū Gardens]] ([[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō, Tokyo]], Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Kenroku-en|Kenroku-en]] ([[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ([[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkyū-en|qq=}} ([[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Katsura Imperial Villa|Katsura Imperial Villa]] Garden ([[:en:Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]) - Managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]] Garden ([[:en:Kita-ku, Kyoto|Kita-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * [[:en:Ginkaku-ji|Ginkaku-ji]] Garden ([[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * [[:en:Tenryū-ji|Tenryū-ji]] Garden ([[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * [[:en:Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)]] Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * [[:en:Nijō Castle|Nijō Castle]] Ninomaru Garden ([[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-in Garden|qq=}} at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] ([[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Adachi Museum of Art|Adachi Museum of Art]] Garden ([[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden Ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * [[Kōraku-en]] ([[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ritsurin Garden|Ritsurin Garden]] ([[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Shikina-en|Shikina-en]] ([[:en:Naha|Naha]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site == See Also == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō garden|qq=}} * [[:en:Water garden|Pond Strolling Garden]]
{{Gardens and Gardening}}
