{{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]] designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] in [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] temples and were extensively developed by [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''[[:en:Water garden|Pond Strolling Garden]]''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/artificial hills|qq=}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ornamental stones|qq=}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as [[:en:Chashitsu|teahouses]] and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/azumaya|qq=}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]] designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] in [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] temples and were extensively developed by [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''[[:en:Water garden|Pond Strolling Garden]]''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/artificial hills|qq=}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ornamental stones|qq=}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as [[:en:Chashitsu|teahouses]] and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/azumaya|qq=}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* [[:en:Koishikawa-Kōrakuen|Koishikawa Kōrakuen]] ([[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Place of Scenic Beauty|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名勝|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Rikugi-en Gardens|Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Hama-rikyū Gardens|Hama-rikyū Gardens]] ([[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō, Tokyo]], Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Kenroku-en|Kenroku-en]] ([[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ([[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkyū-en|qq=}} ([[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Katsura Imperial Villa|Katsura Imperial Villa]] Garden ([[:en:Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]) - Managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]] Garden ([[:en:Kita-ku, Kyoto|Kita-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * [[:en:Ginkaku-ji|Ginkaku-ji]] Garden ([[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * [[:en:Tenryū-ji|Tenryū-ji]] Garden ([[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * [[:en:Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)]] Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * [[:en:Nijō Castle|Nijō Castle]] Ninomaru Garden ([[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-in Garden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] ([[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Adachi Museum of Art|Adachi Museum of Art]] Garden ([[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden Ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * [[:en:Kōraku-en|Kōraku-en]] ([[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ritsurin Garden|Ritsurin Garden]] ([[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Shikina-en|Shikina-en]] ([[:en:Naha|Naha]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site == See Also == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō garden|qq=}} * [[:en:Water garden|Pond Strolling Garden]]

'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]] designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] in [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] temples and were extensively developed by [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''[[:en:Water garden|Pond Strolling Garden]]''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/artificial hills|qq=|1=artificial hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10460934}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ornamental stones|qq=|1=Japanese garden stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7012221}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as [[:en:Chashitsu|teahouses]] and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/azumaya|qq=|1=azumaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378159}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.

'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]] designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] in [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] temples and were extensively developed by [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''[[:en:Water garden|Pond Strolling Garden]]''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/artificial hills|1=artificial hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10460934}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ornamental stones|1=Japanese garden stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7012221}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as [[:en:Chashitsu|teahouses]] and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/azumaya|1=azumaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378159}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Japanischer Wandelgarten]][[ja:回遊式庭園]][[zh:回游式庭园]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11420231]] Wikidata name: Japanese strolling garden [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]] designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] in [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] temples and were extensively developed by [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''[[:en:Water garden|Pond Strolling Garden]]''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/artificial hills|1=artificial hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10460934}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ornamental stones|1=Japanese garden stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7012221}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as [[:en:Chashitsu|teahouses]] and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/azumaya|1=azumaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378159}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==

Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ([[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkyū-en|1=Genkyū-en|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572274}} ([[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]) - <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Japanischer Wandelgarten]][[ja:回遊式庭園]][[zh:回游式庭园]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11420231]] Wikidata name: Japanese strolling garden [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]] designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] in [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] temples and were extensively developed by [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''[[:en:Water garden|Pond Strolling Garden]]''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/artificial hills|1=artificial hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10460934}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ornamental stones|1=Japanese garden stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7012221}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as [[:en:Chashitsu|teahouses]] and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/azumaya|1=azumaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378159}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* [[:en:Koishikawa-Kōrakuen|Koishikawa Kōrakuen]] ([[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名勝|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} * [[:en:Rikugi-en Gardens|Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Hama-rikyū Gardens|Hama-rikyū Gardens]] ([[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō, Tokyo]], Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Kenroku-en|Kenroku-en]] ([[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ([[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkyū-en|1=Genkyū-en|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572274}} ([[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Katsura Imperial Villa|Katsura Imperial Villa]] Garden ([[:en:Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]) - Managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]] Garden ([[:en:Kita-ku, Kyoto|Kita-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * [[:en:Ginkaku-ji|Ginkaku-ji]] Garden ([[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * [[:en:Tenryū-ji|Tenryū-ji]] Garden ([[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * [[:en:Saihō-ji (Kyoto)|Saihō-ji (Kyoto)]] Garden (Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * [[:en:Nijō Castle|Nijō Castle]] Ninomaru Garden ([[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * {{ill|Sambō-in Garden|ja|三宝院庭園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-in Garden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] ([[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Adachi Museum of Art|Adachi Museum of Art]] Garden ([[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) - Ranked first in a Japanese garden Ranking by the Journal of Japanese Gardening * [[:en:Kōraku-en|Kōraku-en]] ([[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ritsurin Garden|Ritsurin Garden]] ([[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Shikina-en|Shikina-en]] ([[:en:Naha|Naha]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty and World Heritage Site == See Also == * {{ill|Daimyō garden|ja|大名庭園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō garden|1=daimyo garden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433351}} * [[:en:Water garden|Pond Strolling Garden]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Japanischer Wandelgarten]][[ja:回遊式庭園]][[zh:回游式庭园]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11420231]] Wikidata name: Japanese strolling garden [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]] designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] in [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] temples and were extensively developed by [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''[[:en:Water garden|Pond Strolling Garden]]''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/artificial hills|1=artificial hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10460934}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ornamental stones|1=Japanese garden stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7012221}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as [[:en:Chashitsu|teahouses]] and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/azumaya|1=azumaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378159}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Japanischer Wandelgarten]][[ja:回遊式庭園]][[zh:回游式庭园]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Japanischer Wandelgarten]][[ja:回遊式庭園]][[zh:回游式庭园]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Japanischer Wandelgarten]][[ja:回遊式庭園]][[zh:回游式庭园]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11420231]] Wikidata name: Japanese strolling garden [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]] designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] in [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] temples and were extensively developed by [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''[[:en:Water garden|Pond Strolling Garden]]''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/artificial hills|1=artificial hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10460934}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ornamental stones|1=Japanese garden stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7012221}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as [[:en:Chashitsu|teahouses]] and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/azumaya|1=azumaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378159}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==

Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ([[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkyū-en|1=Genkyū-en|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572274}} ([[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]) - '''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]] designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] in [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] temples and were extensively developed by [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''[[:en:Water garden|Pond Strolling Garden]]''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/artificial hills|1=artificial hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10460934}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ornamental stones|1=Japanese garden stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7012221}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as [[:en:Chashitsu|teahouses]] and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/azumaya|1=azumaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378159}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* [[:en:Koishikawa-Kōrakuen|Koishikawa Kōrakuen]] ([[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名勝|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} * [[:en:Rikugi-en Gardens|Rikugi-en Gardens]] (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Hama-rikyū Gardens|Hama-rikyū Gardens]] ([[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō, Tokyo]], Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Kenroku-en|Kenroku-en]] ([[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * [[:en:Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins]] ([[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkyū-en|1=Genkyū-en|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572274}} ([[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * [[:en:Ginkaku-ji|Ginkaku-ji]] Garden ([[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - Special Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, and World Heritage Site * [[:en:Tenryū-ji|Tenryū-ji]] Garden ([[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto) - '''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|en|Japanese garden|WD=Q15835|lt=Japanese garden}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} in {{ill|Zen|en|Zen|WD=Q7953|lt=Zen Buddhism}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|Daimyo|en|Daimyo|WD=Q61982|lt=daimyō}} during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Water garden|en|Water garden|WD=Q113574|lt=Pond Strolling Garden}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/artificial hills|1=artificial hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10460934}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ornamental stones|1=Japanese garden stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7012221}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|Chashitsu|en|Chashitsu|WD=Q946790|lt=teahouses}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/azumaya|1=azumaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378159}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa-Kōrakuen|en|Koishikawa-Kōrakuen|WD=Q1778881|lt=Koishikawa Kōrakuen}} ({{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyō, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名勝|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} * {{ill|Rikugi-en Gardens|en|Rikugi-en Gardens|WD=Q1889010|lt=Rikugi-en Gardens}} (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|en|Hama-rikyū Gardens|WD=Q1033333|lt=Hama-rikyū Gardens}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|en|Chūō, Tokyo|WD=Q212704|lt=Chūō, Tokyo}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|en|Kenroku-en|WD=Q998239|lt=Kenroku-en}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}) - {{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|en|Japanese garden|WD=Q15835|lt=Japanese garden}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} in {{ill|Zen|en|Zen|WD=Q7953|lt=Zen Buddhism}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|Daimyo|en|Daimyo|WD=Q61982|lt=daimyō}} during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Water garden|en|Water garden|WD=Q113574|lt=Pond Strolling Garden}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/artificial hills|1=artificial hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10460934}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ornamental stones|1=Japanese garden stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7012221}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|Chashitsu|en|Chashitsu|WD=Q946790|lt=teahouses}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/azumaya|1=azumaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378159}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.

Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|en|Hama-rikyū Gardens|WD=Q1033333|lt=Hama-rikyū Gardens}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|en|Chūō, Tokyo|WD=Q212704|lt=Chūō, Tokyo}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|en|Kenroku-en|WD=Q998239|lt=Kenroku-en}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}) - {{Wikidatabox}} [[File:Katsurarikyu01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Garden of Katsura Imperial Villa]]
'''Kaiyū-shiki Teien''' (回遊式庭園) is a style of {{ill|Japanese garden|en|Japanese garden|WD=Q15835|lt=Japanese garden|1=Japanese garden}} designed for strolling and viewing various landscapes within the garden. While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.
== Overview ==
Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} in {{ill|Zen|en|Zen|WD=Q7953|lt=Zen Buddhism|1=Zen}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|Daimyo|en|Daimyo|WD=Q61982|lt=daimyō|1=Daimyo}} during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. This style is considered a culmination of Japanese garden design.
The most common type is the '''{{ill|Water garden|en|Water garden|WD=Q113574|lt=Pond Strolling Garden|1=Water garden}}''' (池泉回遊式庭園), which features a central pond surrounded by paths. These paths allow visitors to enjoy views of {{ill|artificial hills|ja|築山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/artificial hills|1=artificial hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10460934}}, small islands in the pond, bridges, and {{ill|ornamental stones|ja|庭石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ornamental stones|1=Japanese garden stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7012221}} representing scenic locations. Along the pathways, structures such as {{ill|Chashitsu|en|Chashitsu|WD=Q946790|lt=teahouses|1=Chashitsu}} and {{ill|azumaya|ja|四阿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/azumaya|1=azumaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378159}} were built to serve as rest stops and observation points.
== Representative Kaiyū-shiki Teien ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa-Kōrakuen|en|Koishikawa-Kōrakuen|WD=Q1778881|lt=Koishikawa Kōrakuen|1=Koishikawa-Kōrakuen}} ({{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyō, Tokyo|1=Bunkyō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}) - {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Historic site|WD=Q1081138}} and {{ill|Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|特別名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Place of Scenic Beauty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名勝|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} * {{ill|Rikugi-en Gardens|en|Rikugi-en Gardens|WD=Q1889010|lt=Rikugi-en Gardens|1=Rikugi-en Gardens}} (Bunkyō, Tokyo) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Hama-rikyū Gardens|en|Hama-rikyū Gardens|WD=Q1033333|lt=Hama-rikyū Gardens|1=Hama-rikyū Gardens}} ({{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|en|Chūō, Tokyo|WD=Q212704|lt=Chūō, Tokyo|1=Chūō, Tokyo}}, Tokyo) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Kenroku-en|en|Kenroku-en|WD=Q998239|lt=Kenroku-en|1=Kenroku-en}} ({{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa|1=Kanazawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|en|Ichijōdani_Asakura_Family_Historic_Ruins|WD=Q2968274|lt=Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins|1=Ichijōdani Asakura Family Historic Ruins}} ({{ill|Fukui (city)|en|Fukui (city)|WD=Q201618|lt=Fukui|1=Fukui (city)}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|WD=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture|1=Fukui Prefecture}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Genkyū-en|ja|玄宮園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkyū-en|1=Genkyū-en|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572274}} ({{ill|Hikone, Shiga|en|Hikone, Shiga|WD=Q386635|lt=Hikone|1=Hikone, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}) - Special Historic Site and Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Katsura Imperial Villa|en|Katsura Imperial Villa|WD=Q1736808|lt=Katsura Imperial Villa|1=Katsura Imperial Villa}} Garden ({{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1154588|lt=Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}) - Special Historic Site and Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Adachi Museum of Art|en|Adachi Museum of Art|WD=Q3329561|lt=Adachi Museum of Art|1=Adachi Museum of Art}} Garden ({{ill|Yasugi, Shimane|en|Yasugi, Shimane|WD=Q851114|lt=Yasugi|1=Yasugi, Shimane}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}) - Special Place of Scenic Beauty * {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|en|Ritsurin Garden|WD=Q1071520|lt=Ritsurin Garden|1=Ritsurin Garden}} ({{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu|1=Takamatsu}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}) - While strolling-style gardens exist outside Japan, the term "kaiyū-shiki teien" typically refers to Japanese gardens specifically.

Kaiyū-shiki teien became prominent during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} in {{ill|Zen|en|Zen|WD=Q7953|lt=Zen Buddhism|1=Zen}} temples and were extensively developed by {{ill|Daimyo|en|Daimyo|WD=Q61982|lt=daimyō|1=Daimyo}} during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}.
